refsect1.title: DESCRIPTION
refsect1.para: lxsession provides an X11 session manager. It is the default session manager used by the LXDE desktop environment.

A session manager is responsible for automatically launching a set of core applications, exporting foundational environment variables, initializing configuration databases, and setting up a working desktop environment. Furthermore, upon session exit, it orchestrates lifecycle teardown notifications for active window managers, clipboards, polkit agents, and component loops.

listitem.para: Specify the name of the desktop session profile to use (Defaults to LXDE).
Specify the name of the Desktop Environment being initialized, such as LXDE, GNOME, or XFCE.
Reload existing workspace configurations and re-trigger active Xsettings daemon layouts.
Disable the build-in or configured third-party Xsettings daemon management entirely.
Disable desktop autostart files and applications loading routine. Forces window-manager initialization only mode.
Specifies a fallback compatibility engine layout mapping structure. Currently, razor-qt fallback configuration parses are implemented.

refsect1.title: AUTHOR
refsect1.para: This manual page was originally written by paulliu grandpaul@gmail.com for the Debian system (but may be used by others). It has been expanded and updated to accurately track application parameters for modern desktop releases. Permission is granted to copy, distribute and/or modify this document under the terms of the GNU General Public License, Version 2 or any later version published by the Free Software Foundation.
On Debian systems, the complete text of the GNU General Public License can be found in /usr/share/common-licenses/GPL.
